# Metropolis Ascendant - Full Bonus List

*Auto-generated from the mod's data, so it matches the in-game tech and civic node notes exactly.*

Every scaling bonus is at FULL strength with one settlement per hemisphere and OFF at two or more. Bonuses tier up as your city's Urban Population crosses three thresholds (T1 / T2 / T3); mastery bonuses unlock on a node's Mastery panel.

## Antiquity (Population tiers T1 / T2 / T3 = 5 / 9 / 12)

- **Writing** (Tech): +1 Science adjacency per Tier (max +3); +3 Palace and +2 per-building Great Work slots (T2).
- **Writing** (Tech): +1 Science per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Writing** (Tech): While Joyous or happier: +1 Science per 4 Urban Population, increasing further while Ecstatic.
- **Masonry** (Tech): +20% Wonder Production (T2).
- **Masonry - Mastery** (Tech): +1 Gold and Happiness on Appealing tiles, and +50% to all yields from tiles with a Natural Wonder.
- **Mysticism** (Civic): +1 Culture adjacency per Tier (max +3); +1 Culture per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Mysticism** (Civic): While Joyous or happier: +1 Culture per 4 Urban Population, increasing further while Ecstatic.
- **Currency** (Tech): +1 Specialist slot per district per Tier (max +3, Cities only); +2 Resource capacity (T1-T2, max +4). Specialists also cost 50% less Food and Happiness to maintain until this Settlement is Ecstatic.
- **Wheel** (Tech): +2 Resource capacity (T3; +6 total).
- **Code of Laws** (Civic): +2 Trade Routes, +5 Trade Route range (land and sea), and +Happiness from Resources.
- **Bronze Working** (Tech): +1 Production per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Organized Military** (Civic): +3 Combat Strength in all combat.
- **Military Training** (Tech): +25 District HP, +5 Unit healing per turn, and +1 Gold on Fortifications.
- **Irrigation** (Tech): +2 Food per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +Food on worked Farms, Pastures, Plantations and Fishing Boats.
- **Bronze Working** (Tech): +2 Production per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +Production on worked Camps, Mines, Quarries and Woodcutters.
- **Code of Laws** (Civic): Suzerain bonuses scale with your Population. Draft a city-state's repeatable (Shareable) bonus to gain +1 of its yield per 3 Population (Science/Culture/Production/Gold/Food), or +2 Influence per Suzerain (Diplomatic). Each Suzerain grants +1 Resource capacity; Economic ones add +5 Trade Route range.

## Exploration (Population tiers T1 / T2 / T3 = 8 / 14 / 20)

- **Astronomy** (Tech): +1 Science adjacency per Tier (max +3); +3 Palace and +2 per-building Great Work slots (T2).
- **Astronomy** (Tech): +1 Science per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Astronomy** (Tech): While Joyous or happier: +1 Science per 4 Urban Population, increasing further while Ecstatic.
- **Castles** (Tech): +25% Wonder Production (T2).
- **Castles - Mastery** (Tech): +1 Gold and Happiness on Appealing tiles, and +50% to all yields from tiles with a Natural Wonder.
- **Piety** (Civic): +1 Culture adjacency per Tier (max +3); +1 Culture per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Piety** (Civic): While Joyous or happier: +1 Culture per 4 Urban Population, increasing further while Ecstatic.
- **Guilds** (Tech): +1 Specialist slot per district per Tier (max +3, Cities only); +2 Resource capacity (T1-T2, max +4). Specialists also cost 50% less Food and Happiness to maintain until this Settlement is Ecstatic.
- **Mercantilism** (Civic): +2 Resource capacity (T3; +6 total).
- **Cartography** (Tech): +2 Trade Routes, +5 Trade Route range (land and sea), and +Happiness from Resources.
- **Metallurgy** (Tech): +1 Production per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Authority** (Civic): +3 Combat Strength in all combat.
- **Heraldry** (Tech): +25 District HP, +5 Unit healing per turn, and +1 Gold on Fortifications.
- **Feudalism** (Tech): +2 Food per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +Food on worked Farms, Pastures, Plantations and Fishing Boats.
- **Machinery** (Tech): +2 Production per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +Production on worked Camps, Mines, Quarries and Woodcutters.
- **Theology** (Civic): +2 Great Work slots on Temples (Relics, Codices, Artifacts, Great Works), +2 Happiness per Building, and +1 Culture per Great Work in this city (T2).
- **Diplomatic Service** (Civic): Suzerain bonuses scale with your Population. Draft a city-state's repeatable (Shareable) bonus to gain +1 of its yield per 3 Population (Science/Culture/Production/Gold/Food), or +2 Influence per Suzerain (Diplomatic). Each Suzerain grants +1 Resource capacity; Economic ones add +5 Trade Route range.

## Modern (Population tiers T1 / T2 / T3 = 10 / 16 / 24)

- **Academics** (Tech): +1 Science adjacency per Tier (max +3); +4 Palace and +3 per-building Great Work slots (T2).
- **Academics** (Tech): +1 Science per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Academics** (Tech): While Joyous or happier: +1 Science per 4 Urban Population, increasing further while Ecstatic.
- **Steam Engine** (Tech): +30% Wonder Production (T2).
- **Steam Engine - Mastery** (Tech): +1 Gold and Happiness on Appealing tiles, and +50% to all yields from tiles with a Natural Wonder.
- **Natural History** (Civic): +1 Culture adjacency per Tier (max +3); +1 Culture per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Natural History** (Civic): While Joyous or happier: +1 Culture per 4 Urban Population, increasing further while Ecstatic.
- **Electricity** (Tech): +1 Specialist slot per district per Tier (max +3, Cities only); +3 Resource capacity (T1-T2, max +6). Specialists also cost 50% less Food and Happiness to maintain until this Settlement is Ecstatic.
- **Electricity** (Tech): +3 Resource capacity (T3; +9 total).
- **Combustion** (Tech): +3 Trade Routes, +5 Trade Route range (land and sea), and +Happiness from Resources.
- **Military Science** (Tech): +1 Production per 2 Urban Population (T3).
- **Nationalism** (Civic): +3 Combat Strength in all combat.
- **Military Science** (Tech): +25 District HP, +5 Unit healing per turn, and +1 Gold on Fortifications.
- **Mass Production** (Tech): +2 Food per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +Food on worked Farms, Pastures, Plantations and Fishing Boats.
- **Industrialization** (Tech): +2 Production per settlement under your Settlement Cap, and +Production on worked Camps, Mines, Quarries and Woodcutters.
- **Globalism** (Civic): Suzerain bonuses scale with your Population. Draft a city-state's repeatable (Shareable) bonus to gain +1 of its yield per 3 Population (Science/Culture/Production/Gold/Food), or +2 Influence per Suzerain (Diplomatic). Each Suzerain grants +1 Resource capacity; Economic ones add +5 Trade Route range.

## Foundations

Always on, no research needed: bonus Happiness and reduced specialist upkeep that grow with your city, plus +3 Influence per turn to help you win your first city-states.
